commitments.expected_result: runtime accepts only current structured result bodies or reissues the current packet
commitments.failure_boundary: unsupported fields, prose, stale packets, and aliases are rejected
commitments.label: Current packet results use only current result contracts.
commitments.rationale: Runtime must fail closed on unsupported result shapes.

commitments.expected_result: runtime accepts only a supported executable repair plan with current producer/action evidence and rejects retired packet_reissue or replacement fields
commitments.failure_boundary: no old package, replacement-packet branch, fallback cache, alias, or prose result may become success
commitments.label: Repair stays on one current executable plan and package/result/blocker path.
commitments.rationale: Repeated and stale submissions are audit records, not alternate success paths.
commitments.trigger: a current control blocker requires PM repair or a current packet result requires correction
commitments.validation_boundary: current executable plan and packet/result/blocker repair state

commitments.actor: worker, FlowGuard operator, or reviewer role
commitments.expected_result: runtime requires and relays pm_visible_summary but never synthesizes semantic content
commitments.failure_boundary: missing summary blocks mechanically; summary text never substitutes for sealed body review
commitments.label: PM-visible summaries are role-authored current fields.
commitments.rationale: Runtime can require the field but cannot perform semantic reviewer quality checks.
commitments.trigger: formal non-PM role result body is submitted

commitments.expected_result: assigned role receives and uses the authorized current material before submit-result
commitments.failure_boundary: recent summaries and ordinary file access do not satisfy required sealed-body reads
commitments.label: Authorized result reads are required material, not optional summaries.
commitments.rationale: This keeps summary navigation separate from formal evidence consumption.
commitments.validation_boundary: authorized material opening and receipt validation

commitments.expected_result: all, adversarial, and release proof compiles before strict parents, with repository final confidence downstream
commitments.failure_boundary: stale proof, deferred release suites, or a final-confidence/TestMesh/MTA cycle blocks broad claims
commitments.label: Release and done claims require current model/test/install evidence.
commitments.rationale: A project upgrade record is not itself validation proof.
commitments.trigger: maintenance, done, release, or publish confidence is claimed

commitments.expected_result: submission_checklist.v2 is a fingerprinted projection of current_handoff_contract.v2
commitments.failure_boundary: packet bodies, reissue diagnostics, old schemas, aliases, and remembered registry shapes cannot authorize a result
commitments.label: Open-packet checklist has one mechanical contract authority.
commitments.rationale: A role must never choose between body, envelope, registry, or remembered contract shapes.
commitments.trigger: ACKed current packet is opened for submission

commitments.expected_result: declared, selected, executed, passed, failed, and not-run cases remain separate and selected rows carry real submit_result assertions
commitments.failure_boundary: generated payloads, model cells, progress output, or hard-coded statuses cannot count as executed passes
commitments.label: Fake-AI coverage uses finite universes and real submit receipts.
commitments.rationale: The coverage claim is bounded but every selected cell must be observed, not inferred.
commitments.trigger: AI-response coverage or release confidence is reported
commitments.validation_boundary: finite mechanical universe and selected public submit execution

commitments.expected_result: the child supplies final command, result path, exit code, selected/executed counts, covered ids, and artifact fingerprints
commitments.failure_boundary: not-run, stale, progress-only, failed, missing-exit, or proof-free rows remain blocking
commitments.label: TestMesh green requires current fingerprinted proof artifacts.
commitments.rationale: A model-authored green label cannot substitute for an executed child proof.
commitments.trigger: a child suite is consumed by routine, done, release, or publish confidence

commitments.expected_result: the current checklist projects all active acceptance items onto a route owner and supplemental repair item
commitments.failure_boundary: static example ids or missing owner projection mechanically block and cannot enter an endless reissue loop
commitments.label: Terminal PM repair owns every current acceptance item.
commitments.rationale: This closes the observed acc-003 model miss without accepting a compatibility fallback.
commitments.trigger: terminal backward replay creates a repair decision packet

commitments.actor: project manager, worker, reviewer, or FlowGuard operator
commitments.expected_result: the role understands the bounded assignment, writes a numbered local plan, executes it, integrates delegated work, verifies and repairs the artifact, and reports each step with current evidence
commitments.failure_boundary: a quick isolated reply, vague plan, incomplete required step, unintegrated delegation, stale evidence, or contradicted completion remains Reviewer-visible failure and routes through the existing PM repair path
commitments.label: Every substantive AI role owns a complete bounded workstream.
commitments.rationale: A FlowPilot node is a complete accountable AI workstream inside the PM-owned route boundary.
commitments.trigger: a current substantive role packet is opened
commitments.validation_boundary: bounded role workstream and semantic completion evidence

commitments.expected_result: Controller executes the current mechanical action and never authors a substantive project or role plan
commitments.failure_boundary: Controller planning, reviewing, researching, or inventing route work is rejected as authority leakage
commitments.label: Controller follows only the Runtime-derived foreground action ledger.
commitments.rationale: Controller remains an execution shell while substantive roles receive full workstream autonomy.
commitments.trigger: FlowPilot requests the next foreground action

commitments.expected_result: Runtime records current local skill paths and availability without deep reading; PM selects relevant candidates, and only selected skills enter the existing skill-standard obligation path
commitments.failure_boundary: missing inventory, Runtime semantic selection, or deep reading every skill violates the single discovery path
commitments.label: A mandatory shallow local capability inventory precedes PM skill selection.
commitments.rationale: Local skill discovery remains mandatory but its semantic relevance decision stays with PM.
commitments.trigger: the current run enters preplanning discovery
commitments.validation_boundary: current packet-only capability inventory and selected-skill handoff

commitments.expected_result: PM issues ordinary bounded task.node work and existing FlowGuard/Reviewer gates assess its process and quality
commitments.failure_boundary: no dedicated material-scan form, material-sufficiency state, mandatory material card, or parallel review authority may advance the current path
commitments.label: Material reading and research use ordinary PM role packages.
commitments.rationale: Material work varies by project and should not be a universal special stage.
commitments.trigger: the project needs reading, research, experiment, source verification, or material synthesis
commitments.validation_boundary: ordinary role package plus existing process and quality gates

commitments.expected_result: Reviewer compares every numbered step, delegation, integration, verification, repair, and blocker claim with the actual artifact; every score below nine receives an explicit PM disposition without becoming an automatic Runtime blocker
commitments.failure_boundary: unchecked self-report, silent low score, or Runtime semantic scoring cannot authorize the next gate
commitments.label: Reviewer audits reported plan completion against actual evidence.
commitments.rationale: The report makes plan discipline auditable without turning prose quality into a brittle Runtime schema gate.
commitments.trigger: a substantive role result enters its current review window
commitments.validation_boundary: semantic quality review and current PM repair decision

commitments.actor: any substantive role using role-local FlowGuard
commitments.expected_result: role-local evidence is integrated into the report while the independent formal FlowGuard and Reviewer gates remain authoritative
commitments.failure_boundary: a role-local pass cannot replace, weaken, or pre-satisfy the formal independent gates
commitments.label: Role-local FlowGuard improves work but cannot self-approve it.
commitments.rationale: FlowGuard can be used by every role without collapsing independent oversight.
commitments.trigger: the role uses FlowGuard to plan, model, inspect, or repair its own workstream
commitments.validation_boundary: independence of role-local assistance and formal approval

ledger.project_boundary: FlowPilot current-contract runtime, prompts, models, tests, and install sync
ledger.rationale: Registers the user-visible no-fallback and evidence freshness promises before broad confidence claims.

groups.rationale: Role-authored summary is a required current output field, not runtime-authored semantics.
PM packet summary context is a derived navigation aid, not a decision substitute.
Authorized material reads define required current input material.
Runtime owns the mandatory shallow packet snapshot; PM owns relevance selection and the existing skill-standard handoff.
The report is visible in every substantive role result but remains a semantic Reviewer concern, not a Runtime score.
Material work returns to ordinary PM task packages; old discovery fields remain negative evidence only.
The single current repair path requires a supported executable plan and current producer/action evidence; replacement-packet authority remains negative only.
Retired names may appear only as negative fixtures or historical labels.
One envelope contract owns mechanics; the checklist is its identity-bound projection.
Body/reissue mirrors, conditional body mechanics, and private helper shapes are rejected or deleted instead of preserved as compatibility surfaces.
Retired identities and daemon replay remain negative evidence only.

plan.notes: Current handoff/checklist fields have one owner; body mirrors, conditional body mechanics, private helper shapes, role aliases, and daemon replay are blocked or deleted old paths.
